steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage has an opening em-dash before 'first published in ______ is essential reading.' An opening em-dash signals a parenthetical aside that must be closed by a matching em-dash. The blank must supply the year plus the closing punctuation that completes the parenthetical.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 1689—: Closes the em-dash parenthetical: '—first published in 1689—is essential reading.' The aside is complete and the main clause reads smoothly. ✓
  • Choice B — 1689: No closing dash. The aside opened by the first em-dash is never closed, leaving the sentence structurally broken. ✗
  • Choice C — 1689,: A comma cannot close an em-dash parenthetical. The opening dash has no matching close. ✗
  • Choice D — 1689:: A colon cannot close an em-dash parenthetical. The structure remains open. ✗
A is correct.
